tldr: Move transactional email from Brevo to Amazon SES for lower per-message cost and tighter integration with an existing AWS footprint, accepting that you give up Brevo's marketing/CRM tooling.
why_switch: SES per-message pricing is typically lower at higher transactional volumes
Native fit with AWS IAM, CloudWatch, SNS, and Lambda
Strong deliverability infrastructure when DNS is configured correctly
what_to_export: Verified senders and authenticated domains in Brevo
DKIM/SPF/DMARC DNS records currently set for Brevo
Marketing lists, segments, and contact attributes
Webhook configurations for delivery, bounce, open, click
API keys and the code paths that call them
Automation workflows (note these have no SES equivalent)
export_method: Use Brevo's official APIs/dashboards to export contacts, templates, and suppression lists, and capture your current DNS records. Consult the Brevo documentation for the up-to-date export endpoints and limits. Download enough event history to satisfy any audit or deliverability needs.
import_method: In AWS, verify your sending domain in SES and publish the SES-issued DKIM CNAMEs alongside your existing SPF/DMARC. Import the suppression list via the SES account-level suppression API, re-create templates using SES template syntax, and configure event publishing through a configuration set into SNS, CloudWatch, or Firehose. Request a sending limit increase before cutover and warm up traffic gradually. Refer to the official Amazon SES documentation for current commands and limits.

concept_mapping.target: IAM user + access key (or SMTP credentials)
concept_mapping.note: Scope IAM policy to SES actions only
SES is delivery only — replace with another tool if needed
SES does not store contact lists

gotchas: SES starts in sandbox mode — request production access early
SES is delivery-only: marketing automations, segments, and CRM features in Brevo have no built-in equivalent
Reputation and IP warm-up are required; do not switch all traffic at once
DKIM keys must be regenerated and re-published — keep both vendors live during transition
Bounce and complaint handling logic must be rewritten against SES's SNS payloads
Confirm SES regional availability matches your compliance requirements

email.subject: Migrating from Brevo to Amazon SES
email.body:
Hi team,

We're planning to move transactional email from Brevo to Amazon SES to reduce per-message cost and tighten integration with our AWS stack.

Scope: transactional sending only. We'll need a separate plan for any marketing automations and CRM features currently living in Brevo, since SES does not cover those.

Steps: verify the domain in SES, publish DKIM/SPF/DMARC, import templates and the suppression list, wire up SNS-based bounce/complaint handling, request a sending limit increase, and warm up traffic gradually while running both providers in parallel.

Estimated effort: 2-5 days plus warm-up. I'll circulate a runbook before we start.

Thanks
